Applications open / cohort 01

Build what India
hasn’t built yet.

A one-month deep-tech residency for 16 scientists, engineers, researchers, and builders working across biology, physics, chemistry, nanomaterials, particles, space science, and scientific infrastructure.

Explore the residency↓
NMT—E310⁻⁹ m
ION FIELD0.62 T
STATESTABLE
Date01—30 September 2026
Residents16
EnvironmentOne villa
LocationIndia
02Mission / collective intelligenceVH—2026

A house for people building at the edge of what is possible.

Variance House brings together 16 deeply curious builders to live, experiment, collaborate, and create ambitious scientific projects for one month.

The objective is simple: create the conditions for foundational technologies—and the scientific infrastructure India needs next—to emerge between disciplines.

16
individual depthshared environmentproductive interference
Field exposure / archive state approaching
VH / FIELD INDEX
03Research Tracks / document 018 territories

Sixteen people. Thirty days. Multiple frontiers.

Eight research territories, deliberately placed in one shared environment. The point is not parallel work. It is productive interference.

cell field / 40×
01 / BIO—A1Cellular systems

Biology

Wet lab, synthetic biology, and living materials.

Scale 10 μmState / viable
field trace / 0.62 T
02 / PHY—B4Field dynamics

Physics

Quantum systems, optics, thermodynamics, and matter.

λ 632.8 nmError ±0.04
sensor array / APH—C2
03 / APH—C2Instrumentation

Applied Physics

Sensors, energy systems, control, and imaging.

Input 2.8 VSignal / clean
reaction space / C—12
04 / CHM—D8Reaction space

Chemistry

Catalysis, electrochemistry, and molecular design.

pH 7.18Temp 296 K
surface lattice / 10⁻⁹ m
05 / NMT—E3Material structure

Nanomaterials

Surfaces, structures, fabrication, and coatings.

Ra 0.14 μmDensity 2.18
event trace / high energy
06 / PAR—F6High energy

Particle Science

Detection, radiation, and fundamental matter.

Event 0142σ 2.3
orbital system / SPC—G5
07 / SPC—G5Orbital systems

Space Science

Payloads, remote sensing, and propulsion.

shared systems / India

Scientific Infrastructure

Tools, labs, shared systems, and access.

Node count 16Access / shared
0108
[1] Tracks are starting conditions, not boundaries.Collaboration is expected to alter the initial field state.P.03
04Residency protocol / document 0230 days

The month is
the instrument.

A programme designed around sustained attention, shared tools, and productive interference.

16 residents / 01 villa / 30 days
Seq.Operating conditionExpected effect
P—01

Live together in one villa

Remove the commute between a question and the person who may unlock it.

P—02

Independent + collaborative research

Protect deep individual work while designing for collision across fields.

P—03

Daily experiments

Make, measure, record, alter. Progress is an observed state change.

P—04

Technical discussions

Reason in public. Invite rigorous disagreement before certainty hardens.

P—05

Shared tools + knowledge

Treat instruments, methods, and domain fluency as common infrastructure.

P—06

Weekly demonstrations

Expose unfinished work to the house; let evidence redirect the next week.

P—07

Final research showcase

Present the prototype, result, failed assumption, and next experiment.

Field map / Sept. 2026
30days / continuous
Daily
Experiment + document
Weekly
Demonstrate + recalibrate
Final
Publish + exhibit

The schedule stays porous. The standards do not: contribute, document, share, and build.

01 September / orientationDay 01 of 3030 September / showcase
0107142130
05Signature experiment / material ascentScroll + pointer

An experiment
is a way up.

Questions enter as intuition. Each measured rung turns them into evidence, apparatus, and new infrastructure.

Z 04.72
ascent vector
+0.018 / frame light exposure
4260 K
Δ 0.62
Specimen / NMT—E3

Layered composite
10⁻⁹ m surface study

Molecule / C—12ROT. 0.08°
Aperture / SPC—G5
Material archive / 04

Texture becomes data when every surface is measured.

roughness 0.14 μm / density 2.18 g/cm³ / reflectance 68%
Cursor / tilt ±8°Scroll / ascent ZViewport / recomposeGPU transforms / reduced-motion safe
06Life inside the house / physical archiveSeptember 2026

The work does not end at the lab door.

For thirty days, the villa becomes a living scientific instrument—part workshop, part forum, part home.

Biology × engineeringPhysics × materialsSpace science × softwareAlways in progress
07 Selection Dossier / 2026

A residential environment for practitioners with evidence of sustained work and a method ready to be shared.

Selection principle

Not spectators.
Practitioners.

Bring a difficult physical-world problem, evidence of sustained work, and a method you are ready to share.

A—01Contribution record

Scientists + researchers

Working through evidence, experiments, and unresolved questions.

Evidence / experiment
A—02Builder record

Engineers + technical builders

Turning physical principles into tools, instruments, and systems.

Systems / hardware
A—03Academic depth

Exceptional students

With unusual technical depth and a body of self-directed work.

Depth / work
A—04Self-directed

Independent investigators

Pursuing important work before an institution has made room for it.

Frontier / initiative
A—05Physical world

Physical-world problem solvers

Already committed to a difficult problem—not merely looking to attend.

Commitment / application
05 Bangalore Field Station / City Context 12.9716° N / 77.5946° E
16 People · 30 Days · Bangalore

A FIELD STATION FOR INDIA’S SCIENTIFIC FRONTIER.

Variance House brings scientists, engineers, researchers, and technical builders into one shared Bangalore residence for a month of experiments, collaboration, and difficult physical-world work.

Apply for Bangalore residency↗
